# Unity Master Skill Library

## 概述

本扩展当前提供 **34 个专业技能 + 12 个子代理**，覆盖关卡抽取、试玩广告制作、游戏开发工作流、开发进化分析、AI 资产生成、AI 资产生产、UI 系统、环境渲染、游戏机制九大领域，为 Unity 开发提供全方位专家指导。同时内置 **40+ 篇** 调试经验文档、试玩广告模板和常用代码模板。

## 技能分类

### 关卡抽取 (Level Extract)

从 Unity 项目中抽取指定关卡，移除网络/广告/统计/存档/远程配置等依赖，生成纯单机试玩广告版本。

| Skill                     | 名称               | 说明                                                                                   |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `level-extract-planner`   | 关卡抽取规划器     | 根据需求文档分析项目结构，生成模块化执行计划（ExecutionPlan.md），将改造拆解为独立模块 |
| `level-extract-developer` | 关卡抽取开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的 ExecutionPlan.md，逐模块执行改造，每模块通过编译门禁并 git commit  |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                 | 说明                                        |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| `editor-plan-agent`   | 改造规划子代理 - 生成/更新 ExecutionPlan.md |
| `editor-module-agent` | 改造执行子代理 - 执行具体模块并通过编译门禁 |

适用场景：试玩、抽关、关卡抽取、playable ads、微信试玩、抖音试玩、单关卡版本、离线版本

**工作流**：

```text
用户提供需求文档 → level-extract-planner → ExecutionPlan.md → level-extract-developer → 改造完成
```

### 试玩广告制作 (Playable Ad)

| Skill                   | 名称               | 说明                                                                                                         |
| ----------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `playable-ad-planner`   | 试玩广告规划器     | 根据游戏类型和现有素材，规划 30 秒试玩广告的完整流程（开始页→游戏玩法→结算页），生成资产需求清单和开发教程   |
| `playable-ad-developer` | 试玩广告开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，优先使用现有资产，无可用资产时调用 TJGenerator 生成，快速实现 30 秒试玩广告体验 |

### 游戏开发工作流 (Game Dev Workflow)

| Skill                | 名称           | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------- | -------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `game-dev-planner`   | 游戏教程规划器 | 根据游戏类型生成 AI 可执行的开发教程和资产需求清单，交接给 developer |
| `game-dev-developer` | 游戏开发执行器 | 消费 planner 产出的交接产物，先生成资产再直接开发游戏                |
| `game-dev-debugger`  | 游戏调试执行器 | 检索开发经验知识库，为游戏开发/生成过程中的调试问题提供支持          |

### 开发进化分析 (Game Evolution)

| Skill              | 名称           | 说明                                                                                              |
| ------------------ | -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `log-analyzer`     | 日志分析器     | 从开发会话日志中提取 bug、失败模式和可复用经验，去重后自动生成经验文档写入知识库                  |
| `session-profiler` | 会话性能分析器 | 解析聊天日志，统计工具调用次数与失败率、Skill 激活、Sub-Agent 调度、耗时分布，生成 Profiling 报告 |

### AI 资产生成 (TJGenerators)

集成团结 AI 平台的多模态生成能力，无缝嵌入 Unity 编辑器工作流。

| Skill                            | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| -------------------------------- | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `generate_3d_model`              | 3D 模型生成        | 生成静态 3D 模型；默认优先 Tripo P1，高精度 / PBR / FBX 场景可切换腾讯混元 3D |
| `generate_animated_character`    | 动画角色生成       | 生成带骨骼动画的 Humanoid 角色                                       |
| `generate_rigged_animated_model` | 绑骨动画模型生成   | 为现有模型绑骨和/或生成动作动画                                      |
| `generate_sprite`                | 精灵生成           | 生成 2D 精灵资产（图标、物品图、角色肖像等）                         |
| `generate_sprite_sequence`       | 精灵序列生成       | 从角色参考图生成 2D 逐帧动画                                         |
| `generate_image`                 | 图片生成           | 生成 Texture2D / PNG 图片资产（概念图、UI 背景等）                   |
| `generate_material`              | 材质生成           | 生成 PBR 表面材质（无缝纹理 PNG + `.mat` 文件）                      |
| `generate_skybox`                | 天空盒生成         | 生成天空盒 Cubemap / Skybox 材质                                     |
| `generate_audio_clip`            | 背景音乐生成       | 生成 BGM 和环境音频片段                                              |
| `generate_sound_effect`          | 音效生成           | 生成一次性音效（枪声、脚步声、爆炸等）                               |
| `generate_terrain`               | 地形生成           | 生成大尺度 Unity Terrain 地形并应用到当前场景                        |
| `generate_video`                 | 视频生成           | 生成视频资产（宣传视频、转场视频、场景视频等）                       |
| `search_assets`                  | 资产搜索下载       | 从云端资产库搜索并下载现成资产（搜索→评估→下载）                     |

**子代理（agents/）**：

| Agent                                  | 说明                                         |
| -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `ai-generation-planner`                | AI 生成规划子代理 - 多任务生成请求拆解与执行规划 |
| `3d-model-generator`                   | 3D 模型生成子代理                            |
| `animated-character-generator`         | 动画角色生成子代理                           |
| `audio-generator`                      | 音频生成子代理                               |
| `image-generator`                      | 图片生成子代理                               |
| `material-generator`                   | 材质生成子代理                               |
| `skybox-generator`                     | 天空盒生成子代理                             |
| `sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator` | 精灵及序列生成子代理                         |
| `search-assets`                        | 资产搜索与下载子代理                         |
| `video-generator`                      | 视频生成与 VideoPlayer 配置子代理            |

### AI 资产生产 (AI Asset Production)

面向已有资产的接线、放置、地形/骨骼后处理与场景整合。

| Skill                    | 名称               | 说明                                                                 |
| ------------------------ | ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `fbx-humanoid-auto-rig`  | FBX Humanoid 自动绑骨 | 使用 UniRig 为任意人形 FBX 自动生成骨架、Humanoid 命名、Skin 与 UV merge |
| `place_assets_in_scene`  | 场景放置专家       | 将已有资产按自然语言要求放入当前场景，支持 Prefab / Sprite / Audio / Material / TerrainData 等 |

### UI 系统 (UI Systems)

| Skill              | 名称            | 说明                                                  |
| ------------------ | --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| `ui-system-router` | UI 系统路由器   | 判断合适的 UI 系统并路由到正确的专业技能              |
| `imgui`            | IMGUI 专家      | 为编辑器扩展和调试工具生成即时模式 GUI 代码           |
| `ugui`             | uGUI 专家       | 理解现有 UI、进行针对性编辑、生成新的 Canvas 层级结构 |
| `ui-toolkit`       | UI Toolkit 专家 | 生成 UXML/USS 文件、Manipulators、处理 UI 运行时绑定  |

### 环境渲染 (Environment & Rendering)

| Skill           | 名称                 | 说明                                            |
| --------------- | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `cinemachine`   | Cinemachine 3.1 专家 | 使用模块化组件系统创建正确的摄像机设置          |
| `lighting`      | Lighting 专家        | 为 Built-in 和 URP 管线创建、配置和排查场景光照 |
| `scene-creator` | Scene Creator 专家   | 以编程方式构建、验证和修复 2D/3D 场景           |

### 游戏机制 (Gameplay Mechanics)

| Skill          | 名称             | 说明                               |
| -------------- | ---------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `2d-character` | 2D 角色专家      | 端到端处理角色创建工作流           |
| `auto-wire`    | 资产自动接线专家 | 自动将生成的资产接入用户项目       |
| `input-system` | 输入系统专家     | 确定合适的系统并实现健壮的输入设置 |

## 经验知识库

### 关卡抽取需求文档 (experience/templates-level-extract/)

| 模板           | 说明                                                                                             |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `LevelExtract` | 关卡抽取试玩广告的标准需求文档模板，包含业务需求约束：纯单机、无存档、无广告、无统计、无远程配置 |

### 试玩广告模板 (experience/templates-playable-ad/)

| 模板                    | 说明                     |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------ |
| `playable_start_page`   | 标题、按钮动画、场景切换 |
| `playable_guide_system` | 目标点、箭头、完成检测   |
| `playable_end_page`     | 分数展示、重玩按钮、微信通知 |

### 调试经验 (experience/development/)

当前内置 **30+ 篇** Unity 开发调试经验文档，涵盖常见问题：

- **UI 问题**：UGUI 遮挡 Camera、EventSystem 缺失导致点击失效、中文/字体显示异常
- **物理问题**：CapsuleCollider 中心漂移、Collider 未设 `isTrigger`、Player 插入地面
- **编译错误**：DLL 访问冲突、Library 缓存错误、匿名类型数组 `CS0826`、私有方法访问
- **渲染问题**：Sprite 半透明边缘、粒子材质显示方块、Standard Shader 光泽度无效
- **运行时问题**：场景对象被禁用、自动射击未实现、相机跟随抖动、游戏状态管理缺失

### 代码模板 (experience/templates/)

| 模板                 | 说明       |
| -------------------- | ---------- |
| `asset_loader`       | 资产加载器 |
| `camera_follow`      | 相机跟随   |
| `collectible_system` | 收集系统   |
| `procedural_meshes`  | 程序化网格 |
| `ui_panels`          | UI 面板    |

## 使用方式

```bash
# 激活特定技能
activate_skill("<skill-name>")

# 关卡抽取
activate_skill("level-extract-planner")
activate_skill("level-extract-developer")

# 试玩广告
activate_skill("playable-ad-planner")
activate_skill("playable-ad-developer")

# 游戏开发工作流
activate_skill("game-dev-planner")
activate_skill("game-dev-developer")
activate_skill("game-dev-debugger")

# AI 资产生成
activate_skill("generate_3d_model")
activate_skill("generate_animated_character")
activate_skill("generate_rigged_animated_model")
activate_skill("generate_sprite")
activate_skill("generate_sprite_sequence")
activate_skill("generate_image")
activate_skill("generate_material")
activate_skill("generate_skybox")
activate_skill("generate_audio_clip")
activate_skill("generate_sound_effect")
activate_skill("generate_terrain")
activate_skill("generate_video")
activate_skill("search_assets")

# AI 资产生产
activate_skill("fbx-humanoid-auto-rig")
activate_skill("place_assets_in_scene")

# 开发进化
activate_skill("log-analyzer")
activate_skill("session-profiler")

# Unity 专家
activate_skill("lighting")
activate_skill("cinemachine")
activate_skill("scene-creator")
activate_skill("ui-system-router")
```

## 技能依赖关系

```text
level-extract-planner → level-extract-developer
editor-plan-agent → editor-module-agent (关卡抽取子代理)

playable-ad-planner → playable-ad-developer

game-dev-planner → game-dev-developer → game-dev-debugger → log-analyzer → session-profiler

ai-generation-planner → 3d-model-generator | animated-character-generator | audio-generator | image-generator | material-generator | skybox-generator | sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator | search-assets | video-generator

generate_3d_model → place_assets_in_scene
generate_animated_character → place_assets_in_scene
generate_sprite → place_assets_in_scene
generate_terrain → place_assets_in_scene
search_assets → search-assets → place_assets_in_scene
generate_video → video-generator

2d-character → auto-wire
ui-system-router → imgui | ugui | ui-toolkit
```

## Available Sub-Agents
Use `task` for complex tasks requiring specialized analysis.

- **explore**: Fast read-only codebase search agent for finding files, patterns, and answering questions about the codebase. Use when you need to quickly find files by patterns, search code for keywords, or understand how parts of the codebase work. Specify thoroughness: 'quick' for basic searches, 'medium' for moderate exploration, 'very thorough' for comprehensive analysis.
- **general-purpose**: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you.
- **plan**: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs.
- **3d-model-generator**: Generates static (non-animated) 3D models in Unity using AI (defaults to Tripo P1; use Hunyuan 3.1 for high-poly / PBR / FBX output), then places the prefab into the scene
- **ai-generation-planner**: Planning agent for MULTI-TASK AI generation requests in Unity. Only invoke when the user's request involves TWO OR MORE distinct AI generation tasks (e.g. 'generate a character AND a skybox AND a material'). Do NOT invoke for a single generation task — call the appropriate specialist agent directly instead. When invoked, analyzes requirements, decomposes into sub-tasks, resolves dependencies, and produces a structured execution plan.
- **animated-character-generator**: Generates rigged humanoid 3D characters with animations in Unity using Meshy AI, with automatic rotation correction to ensure the character stands upright and faces the camera
- **audio-generator**: Generates background music (BGM) and sound effects (SFX) in Unity using AI, correctly configures AudioSource settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder audio files
- **image-generator**: Generates image assets (Texture2D PNG) in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images. Supports 火山 SeeDream (default, with size/segmentation options) and Frontier (stylized effects). Use this agent for general-purpose image generation — concept art, UI backgrounds, textures, posters, banners — NOT for game sprites with transparent backgrounds (use sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator instead).
- **material-generator**: Generates PBR surface material assets (seamless PNG texture + Unity .mat file) in Unity using Huoshan SeeDream AI from text prompts, reference images, or material type presets
- **search-assets**: Searches and downloads pre-made Unity assets from the cloud library, then places them in the scene. Use for '搜索资产', 'find a cat model', 'download a spaceship prefab', 'search the asset library for furniture', or any request to find/download existing Unity assets rather than generating new ones.
- **skybox-generator**: Generates Cubemap skybox assets in Unity using Rodin AI from text prompts or reference images, with automatic scene assignment via placeholder workflow
- **sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator**: Handles ALL 2D sprite and sprite sequence tasks in Unity. Three modes — choose based on inputs: Mode A (Sprite only): user wants a static 2D sprite (icon, portrait, UI element, item image), no animation needed; Mode B (Sequence only): user wants a sprite sequence animation AND already has an existing image (image_path is provided); Mode C (Sprite → Sequence): user wants a sprite sequence animation but has NO existing image — generate the sprite first, wait for completion, then use the result as image_path for generate_sprite_sequence. Always use this agent for any combination of sprite and/or sprite sequence generation.
- **video-generator**: Generates video assets in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images, correctly configures VideoPlayer settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder video files
- **editor-module-agent**: 通用 Unity 项目改造 MODULE 子代理 - 按模块使用 Unity 工具与必要的脚本改动完成任务，在不违反 global_requirements_md 约束的前提下通过 Console focus→clear→read 门禁确保编译 0 error，并用 complete_task 回传 handoff_json
- **editor-plan-agent**: 通用 Unity 项目改造 PLAN 子代理 - 严格依照 global_requirements_md 生成模块化 ExecutionPlan.md


## Task Tool (Subagent Dispatch)

The `task` tool is the primary way to launch subagents for complex, multi-step tasks.
- For a single straightforward delegation, call `task` directly without creating a job.
- When a task corresponds to an existing planned job, pass `job_id` so the Task and Job systems stay linked.
- For simple, directed codebase searches (e.g. for a specific file/class/function), use `glob` or `search_file_content` directly.
- For broader codebase exploration and deep research, use the `task` tool with subagent_name="explore". It is faster and read-only. Use this when a simple search proves insufficient or when your task requires more than 3 queries.
- When the user asks to design, architect, or plan before implementing, use the `task` tool with subagent_name="plan" — it explores the codebase and returns a step-by-step plan with critical files.

Actually, the problem might be more subtle. Let me check what happens after a physics update. Maybe the soldiers are being held in place by the physics simulation because they're colliding with the TowerSlot colliders.

Wait, but the soldier's CircleCollider2D is a trigger. And there are TowerSlot colliders with isTrigger=False. But trigger colliders don't generate collision responses. So the soldiers shouldn't be blocked by TowerSlots.

Let me wait a moment and check the soldier's position again.</think>

messages.content:
<think>The soldier got PUSHED BACK to (9.50, 4.12)! I teleported it to (10.0, 4.5) but it's back at (9.50, 4.12). The velocity is also reset to (0, 0). This means something is actively forcing the soldier back to this position. 

This is very suspicious. Something in the game logic is resetting the soldier's position. Let me think about what could cause this:

1. The Soldier script's `ReturnToRallyPoint` calls `MovePosition` each frame, which sets velocity. But that shouldn't teleport the body BACK to (9.50, 4.12).

2. Wait - `MovePosition` on a Dynamic Rigidbody2D calculates the velocity to reach the target during the next physics step. But what if the `ReturnToRallyPoint` is computing a direction that moves AWAY from the rally point? Let me check:
   - Soldier at (9.50, 4.12)
   - Rally point at (10.18, 4.11)
   - Direction = (10.18 - 9.50, 4.11 - 4.12) = (0.68, -0.01) → normalized ≈ (1, -0.01)
   - newPos = (9.50, 4.12) + (1, -0.01) * 4 * deltaTime ≈ (9.50 + 0.064, 4.12 - 0.0006)
   - So the soldier should move towards the rally point, not away from it.

3. But wait, the soldier was teleported to (10.0, 4.5) and then returned to (9.50, 4.12). That's moving AWAY from the rally point. This doesn't make sense with the ReturnToRallyPoint logic.

Unless... there's something else going on. Maybe the `MovePosition` call is computing a very different target, or maybe there's another part of the code that's resetting the position.

Actually, let me reconsider. The velocity was (2, 0) when I set it manually, but then it was (0, 0) when I checked again. And the position went back to (9.50, 4.12) from (10.0, 4.5). 

This looks like the `MovePosition` is being called EVERY frame from Update(), and when I teleport the soldier, the next frame's MovePosition moves it back! But wait, MovePosition moves TOWARDS the target, not away from it.

Hmm, let me think again. The soldier was at (10.0, 4.5) and needs to go to rally (10.18, 4.11). The direction would be roughly (0.18, -0.39), normalized to approximately (0.42, -0.91). With returnSpeed=4 and deltaTime≈0.016:
newPos = (10.0, 4.5) + (0.42, -0.91) * 4 * 0.016 = (10.0 + 0.027, 4.5 - 0.058) = (10.027, 4.442)

So the soldier should move from (10.0, 4.5) towards (10.027, 4.442), which is towards the rally point. That's NOT back to (9.50, 4.12).

Wait, unless the state changed. When I teleported the soldier, maybe the `ReturnToRallyPoint` check found that the distance to home was < 0.3 (since 10.0 is closer to 10.18 than 9.50), so it switched to Idle state, and then FindEnemy found an enemy and switched to MovingToEnemy, and the MoveTowardsEnemy is moving the soldier somewhere else?

Actually no, the soldiers are all in Returning state with no target.

Let me check if maybe there's a BarracksAttack.AutoAssignTargets that's overriding the state or something. Or maybe the soldier is being repositioned by BarracksAttack.

Actually, I think I should check if there's something in the base class TowerAttack that's affecting the soldier's position. Let me check what `base.Update()` does in BarracksAttack.

Wait, I should also check if maybe the `MovePosition` call in ReturnToRallyPoint is actually computing the wrong target because of a bug. Let me add some debug logging...

But I can't modify the script in Play Mode. Let me stop Play Mode, add debug logging, and restart.

Actually, let me try a different approach. Let me check the actual position of the soldier at each frame by using a coroutine or by directly adding a debug component.

Actually, let me try the simplest thing: stop play mode, fix the underlying issue, and test.

The core problem seems to be that `MovePosition` is being called from `Update()` on a Dynamic Rigidbody2D, but the body isn't moving. This could be because:

1. The Dynamic Rigidbody2D with only a trigger collider doesn't generate collision events, and maybe Unity treats it as "no body to simulate" in some edge case.

2. There might be a physics layer collision issue - the soldier might be on a layer that doesn't interact with itself.

3. The `MovePosition` might need to be called from `FixedUpdate()` for Dynamic Rigidbody2D.

Actually, I just realized something very important. The Unity documentation says:
"Moves the Rigidbody to the specified position by calculating the appropriate linear velocity required to move the Rigidbody to that position during the next physics update."

For Dynamic Rigidbody2D, `MovePosition` works by temporarily setting the velocity. But it's meant to be used during FixedUpdate. If called during Update, the velocity is calculated based on the time to the next physics step, which might be very short (partial frame), resulting in a very large velocity. But this should still move the body.

Actually wait, I just re-read the docs: "If the Rigidbody2D is dynamic then the movement will be performed during the FixedUpdate step."

OK I think the real issue might be simpler. Let me look at the code flow more carefully:

1. Soldier starts in `Idle` state
2. `FindEnemy()` is called - searches from `_homePoint` (rally point) position
3. If an enemy is found within `engageRange`, soldier switches to `MovingToEnemy`
4. In `MovingToEnemy`, `MoveTowardsEnemy()` is called, which uses `MovePosition`
5. When enemy dies or goes out of range, soldier switches to `Returning`
6. In `Returning`, `ReturnToRallyPoint()` is called, which uses `MovePosition`

The soldiers are stuck in `Returning` state. They should be moving towards the rally point. But they're not moving.

Let me check something: when `MovePosition` is called from `Update()`, and it sets the velocity, then during the physics step, the body should move. But what if the `MovePosition` call in `Update()` is being overwritten by the physics simulation?

Actually, I just realized I should check whether the soldier's `Update` method is even being called. The Soldier component is enabled (we checked). Let me verify that the Update loop is running.

Wait, I can't add debug logs in Play Mode. Let me try a different approach - let me add a temporary debug component that will log the soldier's position each frame.

Hmm, but that's complicated. Let me try a simpler approach - let me check if the `MovePosition` works when called from a different context, like a coroutine.

Actually, let me try the simplest fix: instead of using MovePosition, use direct velocity setting in Update/FixedUpdate. For a Dynamic Rigidbody2D, setting velocity directly should work.

Let me stop play mode and fix the code.</think>士兵被传送后立即被拉回 (9.50, 4.12)！有什么在强制重置位置。让我停Play Mode来检查和修复。

response.output:
The user asked me to search for the TowerAttack base class and find soldier position-setting code. I've completed that task and read all three files. Now they want me to analyze the conversation history and provide a summary. Let me check what conversation history there is - this appears to be the first interaction, so there's no prior history to analyze. I should provide a clear summary of what I found and did.Here is the complete summary of the findings:

---

## Completed Task Summary

### 1. TowerAttack Base Class
**File:** `C:\Users\97809\Desktop\sttop5\Assets\Scripts\Tower\TowerAttack.cs`

- **Class declaration:** `public class TowerAttack : MonoBehaviour` (line 8)
- **Namespace:** `TowerDefense.Tower`
- **Purpose:** Base class for all tower attack behaviors. Handles cooldown management, target validation, and dispatches to specific attack methods (`FireProjectile`, `FireMagicAttack`, `FireExplosive`, `SpawnSoldiers`, `FirePierceProjectile`) based on `TowerType`.
- Key virtual methods: `Initialize()`, `Update()`, `CanAttack()`, `GetEffectiveAttackRange()`, `Attack()`
- Note: `SpawnSoldiers()` is a **protected virtual empty stub** — the actual barracks logic is in `BarracksAttack`.

### 2. Soldier Position / Movement Code
**File:** `C:\Users\97809\Desktop\sttop5\Assets\Scripts\Tower\Soldier.cs`

- **`MovePosition` calls (Rigidbody2D):**
  - Line 176: `_rb.MovePosition(newPos);` — inside `MoveTowardsEnemy()`
  - Line 294: `_rb.MovePosition(newPos);` — inside `ReturnToRallyPoint()`
- **Direct `transform.position` assignments:**
  - Line 178: `transform.position = new Vector3(newPos.x, newPos.y, transform.position.z);` — fallback when no Rigidbody (MoveTowardsEnemy)
  - Line 296: `transform.position = new Vector3(newPos.x, newPos.y, transform.position.z);` — fallback when no Rigidbody (ReturnToRallyPoint)
  - Line 344: `transform.position = position;` — inside `Respawn()`
- **Soldier position reads:**
  - Line 150-151: `currentPos = _rb != null ? _rb.position : (Vector2)transform.position;`
  - Line 171-172: same pattern
  - Line 279: same pattern
  - Line 289: same pattern
- **Rally point integration:** Soldier movement is always relative to `_homePoint` (the rally point). Position-setting is used for both chasing enemies and returning to rally point.

### 3. BarracksAttack (the derived TowerAttack for barracks towers)
**File:** `C:\Users\97809\Desktop\sttop5\Assets\Scripts\Tower\BarracksAttack.cs`

- **Inherits from:** `TowerAttack`
- **Soldier spawning:** `SpawnAllSoldiers()` — spawns at `_rallyPoint.position` with radial offset
- **Rally point management:** `MoveRallyPoint()` — clamps new position within tower attack range
- **Soldier respawn:** `RespawnSoldierAfterDelay()` — uses `soldier.Respawn(basePos + offset)` which internally sets `transform.position`
- **Soldier position reads for targeting:**
  - Line 307: `Vector3.Distance(soldier.transform.position, enemy.transform.position)`
- **Other position-related:** Rally point marker positioning, gizmo drawing at rally point position

### Important Notes
- Soldier movement is **not directly controlled by TowerAttack** — instead `BarracksAttack.SpawnAllSoldiers()` creates soldier objects, and each `Soldier` component handles its own movement via `Update()` state machine.
- The `TowerAttack.SpawnSoldiers()` is left empty (line 119 in TowerAttack.cs) — all barracks logic is in the derived `BarracksAttack`.
- The `Soldier` class uses a **dual movement system**: prefers `Rigidbody2D.MovePosition()` when a Rigidbody exists, falls back to direct `transform.position` otherwise.

toolCalls.description:
Launch a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously.

The Task tool launches specialized agents that autonomously handle complex tasks. Each agent has specific capabilities and tools available to it.

Available agents (use these names as the subagent_name value):
- explore: Fast read-only codebase search agent for finding files, patterns, and answering questions about the codebase. Use when you need to quickly find files by patterns, search code for keywords, or understand how parts of the codebase work. Specify thoroughness: 'quick' for basic searches, 'medium' for moderate exploration, 'very thorough' for comprehensive analysis. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- 3d-model-generator: Generates static (non-animated) 3D models in Unity using AI (defaults to Tripo P1; use Hunyuan 3.1 for high-poly / PBR / FBX output), then places the prefab into the scene (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- ai-generation-planner: Planning agent for MULTI-TASK AI generation requests in Unity. Only invoke when the user's request involves TWO OR MORE distinct AI generation tasks (e.g. 'generate a character AND a skybox AND a material'). Do NOT invoke for a single generation task — call the appropriate specialist agent directly instead. When invoked, analyzes requirements, decomposes into sub-tasks, resolves dependencies, and produces a structured execution plan. (Tools: read_file, write_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- animated-character-generator: Generates rigged humanoid 3D characters with animations in Unity using Meshy AI, with automatic rotation correction to ensure the character stands upright and faces the camera (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- audio-generator: Generates background music (BGM) and sound effects (SFX) in Unity using AI, correctly configures AudioSource settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder audio files (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- image-generator: Generates image assets (Texture2D PNG) in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images. Supports 火山 SeeDream (default, with size/segmentation options) and Frontier (stylized effects). Use this agent for general-purpose image generation — concept art, UI backgrounds, textures, posters, banners — NOT for game sprites with transparent backgrounds (use sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator instead). (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- material-generator: Generates PBR surface material assets (seamless PNG texture + Unity .mat file) in Unity using Huoshan SeeDream AI from text prompts, reference images, or material type presets (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- search-assets: Searches and downloads pre-made Unity assets from the cloud library, then places them in the scene. Use for '搜索资产', 'find a cat model', 'download a spaceship prefab', 'search the asset library for furniture', or any request to find/download existing Unity assets rather than generating new ones. (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- skybox-generator: Generates Cubemap skybox assets in Unity using Rodin AI from text prompts or reference images, with automatic scene assignment via placeholder workflow (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator: Handles ALL 2D sprite and sprite sequence tasks in Unity. Three modes — choose based on inputs: Mode A (Sprite only): user wants a static 2D sprite (icon, portrait, UI element, item image), no animation needed; Mode B (Sequence only): user wants a sprite sequence animation AND already has an existing image (image_path is provided); Mode C (Sprite → Sequence): user wants a sprite sequence animation but has NO existing image — generate the sprite first, wait for completion, then use the result as image_path for generate_sprite_sequence. Always use this agent for any combination of sprite and/or sprite sequence generation. (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)

Agent selection guidance:
- For codebase search, file discovery, or answering "where is X" questions, prefer the explore agent instead of general-purpose — it is faster and read-only.
- Use explore for quick searches, code pattern discovery, and understanding how parts of the codebase work.
- Use the plan agent for designing implementation strategies before writing code — it explores the codebase and returns a step-by-step plan with critical files.
- Prefer plan over general-purpose when the user asks to design, architect, or plan before implementing.
- Use general-purpose for multi-step tasks that require writing code, running commands, or performing actions beyond read-only exploration.

When using the Task tool, you must set the subagent_name parameter to one of the agent names listed above (e.g., "general-purpose").

When NOT to use the Task tool:
- If you want to read a specific file path, use the read_file or glob tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly
- If you are searching for a specific class definition like "class Foo", use the glob tool instead, to find the match more quickly
- If you are searching for code within a specific file or set of 2-3 files, use the read_file tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly
- Other tasks that are not related to the agent descriptions above

Usage notes:
- Always include a short description (3-5 words) summarizing what the agent will do
- Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible, to maximize performance; to do that, use a single message with multiple tool uses
- When the agent is done, it will return a single message back to you. The result returned by the agent is not visible to the user. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.
- Optional display name: the name parameter is only for UI/tracking display. If omitted, description is used as the display name.
- You can optionally run agents in the background using the run_in_background parameter, but only for genuinely independent work. When an agent runs in the background, you will be notified when it completes.
- Foreground is the default. Use foreground when you need the agent's results before deciding the next step, especially research that informs implementation.
- Use background only when the work is genuinely independent and can safely complete later, such as long-running tests, build validation, independent audits, or non-overlapping research.
- After launching background tasks, do NOT call `task_output` to poll or block — continue with non-overlapping work or respond to the user. Only check progress if the user explicitly asks or the result was truncated.
- Never guess or summarize background task results before the `<bg_task_done ...>...</bg_task_done>` notification arrives.
- Do not duplicate work on the same files or topic as a running background task.
- If this execution corresponds to an existing planned job, pass job_id so the linked job is auto-claimed and auto-updated on completion/failure.
- IMPORTANT: After launching background tasks, do NOT call `task_output` to poll or block — continue with other work or respond to the user. When a background task finishes, a `<bg_task_done task_id="..." name="..." status="...">result</bg_task_done>` message is automatically injected into the conversation containing the task result. Use this directly, and only use `task_output` if the result was truncated or you need additional details.
- Provide clear, detailed prompts so the agent can work autonomously and return exactly the information you need.
- The agent's outputs should generally be trusted
- Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent
- If the agent description mentions that it should be used proactively, then you should try your best to use it without the user having to ask for it first. Use your judgement.
- If the user specifies that they want you to run agents "in parallel", you MUST send a single message with multiple Task tool use content blocks.

Example usage:

assistant: First let me explore the existing auth implementation.
assistant: Uses the Task tool to launch a general-purpose agent to search for and understand the current authentication code
assistant: Based on the exploration results, I'll now implement the JWT refactoring.
</example>

toolCalls.description:
Launch a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously.

The Task tool launches specialized agents that autonomously handle complex tasks. Each agent has specific capabilities and tools available to it.

Available agents (use these names as the subagent_name value):
- explore: Fast read-only codebase search agent for finding files, patterns, and answering questions about the codebase. Use when you need to quickly find files by patterns, search code for keywords, or understand how parts of the codebase work. Specify thoroughness: 'quick' for basic searches, 'medium' for moderate exploration, 'very thorough' for comprehensive analysis. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- 3d-model-generator: Generates static (non-animated) 3D models in Unity using AI (defaults to Tripo P1; use Hunyuan 3.1 for high-poly / PBR / FBX output), then places the prefab into the scene (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- ai-generation-planner: Planning agent for MULTI-TASK AI generation requests in Unity. Only invoke when the user's request involves TWO OR MORE distinct AI generation tasks (e.g. 'generate a character AND a skybox AND a material'). Do NOT invoke for a single generation task — call the appropriate specialist agent directly instead. When invoked, analyzes requirements, decomposes into sub-tasks, resolves dependencies, and produces a structured execution plan. (Tools: read_file, write_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- animated-character-generator: Generates rigged humanoid 3D characters with animations in Unity using Meshy AI, with automatic rotation correction to ensure the character stands upright and faces the camera (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- audio-generator: Generates background music (BGM) and sound effects (SFX) in Unity using AI, correctly configures AudioSource settings for in-scene playback, and recovers from domain reload by detecting real vs. placeholder audio files (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- image-generator: Generates image assets (Texture2D PNG) in Unity using AI from text prompts or reference images. Supports 火山 SeeDream (default, with size/segmentation options) and Frontier (stylized effects). Use this agent for general-purpose image generation — concept art, UI backgrounds, textures, posters, banners — NOT for game sprites with transparent backgrounds (use sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator instead). (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- material-generator: Generates PBR surface material assets (seamless PNG texture + Unity .mat file) in Unity using Huoshan SeeDream AI from text prompts, reference images, or material type presets (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- search-assets: Searches and downloads pre-made Unity assets from the cloud library, then places them in the scene. Use for '搜索资产', 'find a cat model', 'download a spaceship prefab', 'search the asset library for furniture', or any request to find/download existing Unity assets rather than generating new ones. (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- skybox-generator: Generates Cubemap skybox assets in Unity using Rodin AI from text prompts or reference images, with automatic scene assignment via placeholder workflow (Tools: execute_custom_tool, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, execute_csharp_script, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)
- sprite-and-sprite-sequence-generator: Handles ALL 2D sprite and sprite sequence tasks in Unity. Three modes — choose based on inputs: Mode A (Sprite only): user wants a static 2D sprite (icon, portrait, UI element, item image), no animation needed; Mode B (Sequence only): user wants a sprite sequence animation AND already has an existing image (image_path is provided); Mode C (Sprite → Sequence): user wants a sprite sequence animation but has NO existing image — generate the sprite first, wait for completion, then use the result as image_path for generate_sprite_sequence. Always use this agent for any combination of sprite and/or sprite sequence generation. (Tools: execute_custom_tool, execute_csharp_script, unity_gameobject, unity_scene, unity_asset, unity_menu, unity_console, unity_editor, activate_skill, run_shell_command, read_file, glob, search_file_content, sequential_thinking)

messages.content:
<think>The explore agents found the key files but didn't return the actual contents. Let me read the TowerAttack base class to check if there's something in the Update loop that might be resetting soldier positions. Also, let me check the SoldierData to understand the movement parameters.

Actually, the main mystery is: why does the soldier teleport back to (9.50, 4.12) after I move it? The Soldier.cs code uses MovePosition which should move TOWARDS the rally point, not snap back to the original position.

Let me think about this differently. When I teleported the soldier to (10.0, 4.5), the `ReturnToRallyPoint` method would:
1. Read current pos: `_rb.position` = (10.0, 4.5)
2. Calculate distance to home: Vector2.Distance((10.0, 4.5), (10.18, 4.11)) ≈ 0.42 (still > 0.3)
3. Calculate direction: (10.18-10.0, 4.11-4.5) = (0.18, -0.39), normalized ≈ (0.42, -0.91)
4. Calculate newPos: (10.0 + 0.42*4*dt, 4.5 - 0.91*4*dt)
5. Call MovePosition(newPos)

This should move the soldier CLOSER to the rally point, not back to (9.50, 4.12).

Unless... the problem is that when I set `transform.position`, the Dynamic Rigidbody2D's position gets updated to match, but then during the next physics step, the physics engine moves the body based on velocity, and the velocity was set by a previous MovePosition call.

Wait, let me re-read the MovePosition docs more carefully:
"Moves the Rigidbody to the specified position by calculating the appropriate linear velocity required to move the Rigidbody to that position during the next physics update. During the physics update, the linear velocity is set to the calculated value, the Rigidbody moves, and then the linear velocity is reverted."

So MovePosition:
1. Calculates velocity = (targetPos - currentPos) / fixedDeltaTime
2. Next physics step: sets velocity to that value, body moves, velocity reverts

If MovePosition is called from Update(), and Update runs multiple times between FixedUpdates:
- First Update: MovePosition calculates velocity for target A
- Second Update: MovePosition calculates velocity for target B (slightly different)
- Next FixedUpdate: velocity is set for target B, body moves, velocity reverts

This should work fine. The body moves towards the target.

But what if MovePosition is called from Update, and the physics step happens BETWEEN two Update calls? Then:
- Update 1: MovePosition sets target for velocity
- Physics step: body moves, velocity reverts to (0,0)
- Update 2: MovePosition sets new target
- Physics step: body moves again

This should also work fine.

OK let me try yet another approach. Let me check if maybe the issue is that the soldier is a child of the barracks tower, and when the tower has some position update, it's moving the child soldier back.

Actually, that's a very interesting possibility! Let me check the Soldier_Knight hierarchy - are the soldiers children of the BarracksTower?</think>

toolCalls.name: read_file
toolCalls.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

messages.content:
<think>The TowerAttack base class's `Update()` only handles attack cooldown. Nothing resets soldier positions there.

Let me check the Tower.cs class to see if there's something in the Tower's Update that affects child objects' positions. Also, let me check if the soldiers are children of the tower - if they are, and the tower moves or resets, it would affect the soldiers.

Wait, in BarracksAttack.SpawnAllSoldiers():
```csharp
soldierObj.transform.SetParent(transform);
```

So the soldiers ARE children of the BarracksTower. If the tower's transform changes, the soldiers' world positions would change too. But the tower shouldn't be moving...

Let me check the Tower.cs to see if there's any position logic.

Actually, let me think about this from a different angle. The soldiers are stuck at (9.50, 4.12) and won't move. When I teleport them, they snap back. But I've confirmed that MovePosition IS being called with correct target positions. The issue must be something more fundamental.

Let me check if maybe the Rigidbody2D is being recreated or reset somewhere. Or maybe there's a Tower script that's setting child positions.

Actually, I want to check one more thing. Let me look at how the Tower class handles the soldier movement. The soldiers are children of the BarracksTower. In Unity, when a child has a Dynamic Rigidbody2D, the parent's transform can interfere with the physics simulation.

WAIT. That's it! **Dynamic Rigidbody2D on a child object with a non-identity parent transform scale can cause issues!** When a Dynamic Rigidbody2D is a child of another object, the physics simulation and the transform hierarchy can conflict. The Rigidbody2D's position is in world space, but the transform's position is in local space relative to the parent. If the parent has a non-identity transform (especially scale), this can cause the Rigidbody2D to fight with the transform hierarchy.

More specifically, when you call `MovePosition`, the Rigidbody2D calculates the target in world space, but then the transform hierarchy applies the parent's transform, which can distort the position.

This is a well-known Unity issue. The solution is to either:
1. Not parent the soldier under the tower (or under any object with non-identity transform)
2. Use Kinematic Rigidbody2D and move via transform.position in FixedUpdate
3. Use velocity directly instead of MovePosition

Let me check the BarracksTower's transform to see if there's a scale issue.

Actually, let me also check if maybe the Tower class has an Update that does something with child objects.

But actually, the simplest fix is to just not parent the soldiers under the tower. Let me also check if maybe the tower's scale is not (1,1,1).

Let me look at the Tower.cs script.</think>

messages.content:
<think>Now I have the full picture. Let me analyze the issue:

1. **Tower.cs Update()** calls `FindTarget()` → `FindBestTarget()` → `Attack()` → `_attack.Attack(_currentTarget)`. For barracks, `BarracksAttack.Attack()` spawns soldiers and assigns targets. The soldiers are parented under the tower: `soldierObj.transform.SetParent(transform)`.

2. **The soldiers are children of the BarracksTower.** This is the key issue. When a Dynamic Rigidbody2D is a child of a GameObject that has a non-identity transform, the physics can behave unexpectedly.

Let me think about this more carefully. The TowerSlot objects are placed in the scene. When a tower is placed on a slot, the tower's transform might have a specific position and possibly a scale. The soldiers are then parented under the tower.

Actually, let me think about what happens with the parent-child relationship:

When the soldier is a child of the tower:
- `soldier.transform.position` = world position (affected by parent's transform)
- `rb.position` = world position (independent of parent's transform)
- When you call `rb.MovePosition()`, it sets the world-space target
- The Rigidbody2D moves in world space
- But the transform.localPosition is also updated by the physics engine

Wait, that should work fine. The Rigidbody2D operates in world space regardless of parent.

But there's a subtlety: when the parent has a non-identity scale, the transform conversion between local and world space can cause issues. Specifically:
- `rb.position` returns the world position
- `transform.position` also returns the world position (calculated from localPosition through the parent's transform)
- When the physics engine updates the Rigidbody2D's position, it updates `transform.position` to match
- But if the parent has a non-identity scale, the conversion from world to local can cause distortions

Let me check if the tower (or its parent) has a non-identity scale. I need to start Play Mode to check this...

Actually, let me check the prefab and the TowerSlot script instead. Maybe the tower has a specific scale.

Actually, the most likely issue is this: **the soldiers are parented under the tower, and the tower has a non-identity scale (e.g., the tower sprite is scaled).** This causes the Rigidbody2D on the child to fight with the parent's scale transform, causing the "rubber-banding" effect we observed.

But wait, I can't check this without Play Mode. Let me look at the TowerSlot script to see how towers are placed.

Actually, let me take a different approach. Instead of diagnosing further, let me just fix the core issue: **unparent the soldiers from the tower, or use a different movement approach.**

The simplest fix is to change the Soldier's movement from `MovePosition` to direct velocity setting. For a Dynamic Rigidbody2D with `gravityScale=0`, we can just set `rb.velocity` directly each frame.

Wait, but there's another issue. Setting `rb.velocity` from `Update()` works, but the velocity will be applied during the next physics step. If we set velocity in Update, it should work because the physics step will apply it.

Actually, let me reconsider. The issue might not be parenting at all. Let me look at this from yet another angle.

When I checked, the soldiers had velocity (0,0) and were stuck. But I manually set the velocity to (2,0), and it was set correctly. Then I checked again and the velocity was (0,0) and the position was back to (9.50, 4.12).

This suggests that between my two checks, the physics step ran and:
1. The velocity was applied (the body moved)
2. But then something reset the position back to (9.50, 4.12)

OR:
1. The physics step didn't move the body (even though velocity was non-zero)
2. And then the next Update() called MovePosition which reset the velocity

Wait, I think I understand now. The `MovePosition` documentation says:
"During the physics update, the linear velocity is set to the calculated value, the Rigidbody moves, and then the linear velocity is reverted."

So after a physics step triggered by MovePosition, the velocity is REVERTED to its original value (0,0). That's why the velocity was (0,0) when I checked again.

But what about my manual velocity set? I set `rb.velocity = (2,0)`. Then the next physics step should have moved the body. But when I checked, the position was still (9.50, 4.12) and velocity was (0,0). This means:
1. The body didn't move despite having velocity (2,0) - OR
2. The body DID move but then something reset it

Hmm, let me reconsider the timing. My checks were done from editor scripts which run outside the normal game loop. When I set the velocity, the next check might have happened BEFORE the physics step ran, so the body hadn't moved yet.

But then, the second check showed the position was back at (9.50, 4.12). If the body had moved during the physics step, the position should have changed. Unless the Soldier's Update() method called MovePosition which set a different velocity, and then the physics step moved the body back.

Actually, I think the issue might be even simpler. Let me trace through what happens during a single frame:

1. `Soldier.Update()` is called → `ReturnToRallyPoint()` is called
2. Current position: `_rb.position` = (9.50, 4.12)
3. Home position: `_homePoint.position` = (10.18, 4.11)
4. Direction: (10.18-9.50, 4.11-4.12) = (0.68, -0.01), normalized ≈ (0.9999, -0.0147)
5. newPos = (9.50, 4.12) + (0.9999, -0.0147) * 4 * Time.deltaTime
6. If Time.deltaTime ≈ 0.016: newPos = (9.50 + 0.064, 4.12 - 0.00094) ≈ (9.564, 4.119)
7. `_rb.MovePosition((9.564, 4.119))` is called
8. MovePosition calculates velocity to reach (9.564, 4.119) in one physics step
9. During the next physics step (FixedUpdate), the body moves to (9.564, 4.119) and velocity reverts to (0,0)

This should work! The body should move to (9.564, 4.119). But the fact that it's stuck at (9.50, 4.12) means either:
a. MovePosition isn't actually being called (maybe `_rb` is null?)
b. The physics step isn't running
c. Something is overwriting the position after the physics step

We confirmed `_rb` is not null (it's Dynamic with simulated=True). So (a) is unlikely.

For (b), the physics should be running during play mode. We can see the enemies moving, so physics is working.

For (c), something else might be resetting the position. But what?

Wait - let me re-check the `ReturnToRallyPoint` code:
```csharp
Vector2 currentPos = _rb != null ? _rb.position : (Vector2)transform.position;
float dist = Vector2.Distance(currentPos, (Vector2)_homePoint.position);
if (dist <= 0.3f)
{
    _state = SoldierState.Idle;
    ...
    return;
}

Vector2 retCurrentPos = _rb != null ? _rb.position : (Vector2)transform.position;
Vector2 homePos2D = (Vector2)_homePoint.position;
Vector2 direction = (homePos2D - retCurrentPos).normalized;
Vector2 newPos = retCurrentPos + direction * _data.returnSpeed * Time.deltaTime;
if (_rb != null)
    _rb.MovePosition(newPos);
```

This looks correct. Let me look at `MoveTowardsEnemy`:
```csharp
Vector2 currentPos = _rb != null ? _rb.position : (Vector2)transform.position;
float dist = Vector2.Distance(currentPos, (Vector2)_target.transform.position);
if (dist <= _data.attackRange)
{
    _state = SoldierState.Attacking;
    BlockEnemy();
    return;
}

...

Vector2 moveCurrentPos = _rb != null ? _rb.position : (Vector2)transform.position;
Vector2 targetPos2D = (Vector2)_target.transform.position;
Vector2 direction = (targetPos2D - moveCurrentPos).normalized;
Vector2 newPos = moveCurrentPos + direction * _data.moveSpeed * Time.deltaTime;
if (_rb != null)
    _rb.MovePosition(newPos);
```

This also looks correct.

Hmm, let me think about the BarracksAttack.AutoAssignTargets:
```csharp
private void AutoAssignTargets()
{
    ...
    foreach (var soldier in idleSoldiers)
    {
        ...
        if (bestTarget != null)
        {
            soldier.EngageTarget(bestTarget);
        }
    }
}
```

This only targets idle soldiers. When the soldier is in Returning state, it won't be affected.

OK let me try a completely different approach. Let me change the movement code to use `rb.velocity` directly instead of `MovePosition`, and also unparent the soldiers from the tower. These are the two most likely fixes.

Actually, let me check one more thing first. Let me look at the Soldier_Knight prefab to see if there's anything unexpected on it.</think>让我检查预制件设置并查看可能存在问题的父级缩放。

messages.content: <think>Let me check the prefab's Rigidbody2D settings and also check the Tower/BarracksTower prefab scale. I need to check if the tower has a non-identity scale that could cause the child Rigidbody2D to behave incorrectly.</think>

toolCalls.name: execute_csharp_script
toolCalls.description: Executes a C# script in Unity Editor using Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.Scripting. Captures and returns logs generated during script execution. The C# scripting package must be available in Unity. Supports inline script code or loading from a file path.

messages.content:
<think>**Found the root cause!** Both the BarracksTower and Soldier_Knight prefabs have a scale of (0.20, 0.20, 1.00).

When the soldier is parented under the tower (`soldierObj.transform.SetParent(transform)`), the combined scale becomes:
- Tower world scale: (0.20, 0.20, 1.00)
- Soldier local scale: (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) (relative to parent)
- Soldier world scale: (0.20 * 0.20, 0.20 * 0.20, 1.00 * 1.00) = (0.04, 0.04, 1.00)

This is the classic **Dynamic Rigidbody2D + non-identity parent scale** problem! When a Dynamic Rigidbody2D is a child of a GameObject with a non-identity (especially non-uniform) scale, the physics simulation fights with the transform hierarchy. The Rigidbody2D operates in world space, but the transform conversion between world and local space through a scaled parent causes distortions and "rubber-banding".

Specifically:
1. MovePosition sets a world-space target
2. The physics engine moves the Rigidbody2D to that world position
3. The transform hierarchy then converts this to local space using the parent's scale
4. When the next transform update happens, the world position is recalculated from the local position through the parent's scale
5. This round-trip through the scaled parent causes the position to "snap back" to the original position

The solution is one of:
1. **Don't parent soldiers under the tower** - this avoids the scale issue entirely
2. **Fix the scale** - make sure the tower has scale (1,1,1) and use the sprite's pixelsPerUnit or a child's localScale to control visual size
3. **Use velocity directly** instead of MovePosition - setting `rb.velocity` each frame in FixedUpdate

The simplest and most robust fix is option 1: don't parent the soldiers under the tower. This also makes more sense architecturally - soldiers are independent entities that just need a reference to their rally point, they don't need to be children of the tower.

Let me also consider: the soldiers are at scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) in the prefab. If they're NOT parented under the tower, their world scale will be (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) which is correct. But if they ARE parented under a tower at scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00), their world scale becomes (0.04, 0.04, 1.00) which would make them tiny.

So the fix should be:
1. Remove `soldierObj.transform.SetParent(transform)` from BarracksAttack.SpawnAllSoldiers()
2. Optionally, use `FixedUpdate` instead of `Update` for the movement code
3. Use `rb.velocity` directly for smoother movement

Actually, let me also change the movement approach. Instead of using `MovePosition` (which has quirks), I'll use direct velocity setting. This is simpler and more reliable for a Dynamic Rigidbody2D with gravityScale=0.

Let me fix both Soldier.cs and BarracksAttack.cs:
1. In BarracksAttack: Don't parent soldiers under the tower
2. In Soldier: Use FixedUpdate + rb.velocity for movement instead of Update + MovePosition</think>**找到根因了！** BarracksTower 的 scale 是 (0.20, 0.20, 1.00)，Soldier_Knight 的 scale 也是 (0.20, 0.20, 1.00)。士兵被 `SetParent` 到塔下面，导致组合缩放变成 (0.04, 0.04, 1.00)。Dynamic Rigidbody2D 在非 identity scale 的父对象下会"橡皮筋"弹回！

修复方案：
1. 取消士兵的父子关系（不 `SetParent` 到塔下）
2. 改用 `FixedUpdate` + 直接设置 `velocity` 替代 `Update` + `MovePosition`

3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

args.prompt: Describe what you see in this game screenshot. Is there a tower defense game running? Are there any towers placed? Are there enemies? Is there a barracks tower with soldiers?

response.output:
Based on the screenshot, here is a description of what is happening:

**General Description:**
This is a screenshot of a **tower defense game** played from a top-down perspective. The map features a bright green grassy field with a winding brown dirt path that enemies follow. The path starts at the bottom left, winds up and around in a U-shape, goes right, down, up, and ends at a red square on the far right (likely the player's base).

**Specific Answers to your questions:**

*   **Is there a tower defense game running?**
    **Yes.** The interface at the top confirms this. You can see a gold counter (260), a life/heart counter (20), and a wave indicator in the center showing "1/15" (meaning wave 1 of 15). There is also a button in the top right corner, likely to speed up the game or start the next wave.

*   **Are there enemies?**
    **Yes.** There is a line of **skeletons** walking along the path in the bottom left corner. They are moving in a single file line towards the right.

*   **Is there a barracks tower with soldiers?**
    **Yes.** Just ahead of the skeletons, there is a cluster of grey/white armored figures. These look like **soldiers or knights**. In tower defense games like *Kingdom Rush* (which this resembles), these units are spawned by a **Barracks tower**. They are grouped together and appear to be engaging or blocking the path of the incoming skeletons.

*   **Are there any towers placed?**
    **Yes, but specifically a Barracks.** I do not see any traditional "turret" towers (like archer towers or cannon towers) placed along the rest of the path. The only defensive structure visible is the group of soldiers, which implies a Barracks tower has been placed nearby to spawn them.

messages.content:
<think>Interesting! The soldiers exist and have:
1. `parent=none` — they're unparented correctly!
2. Some have velocity (0.58, 2.94) — they're trying to move!
3. BUT their position is (0,0,0) — they're still at origin!

This is strange. The velocity is non-zero, but the position is (0,0,0). And the soldiers are in MovingToEnemy/Attacking state. 

Wait, the issue might be that when we instantiate the soldier prefab (which has scale 0.20, 0.20, 1.00) at the spawn position, the position is correct at instantiation time, but then something is resetting it.

Actually, looking more carefully: `pos=(0.00, 0.00, 0.00) rbPos=(0.00, 0.00)`. The soldiers are at origin. But they were spawned at the rally point position (which should be near the path). 

Oh wait, I see the problem now! The spawn position in `BarracksAttack.SpawnAllSoldiers()` is calculated from `_rallyPoint.position`. But the rally point is set up in `SetupRallyPoint()` which is called from `Initialize()`. Let me check if the rally point was set up correctly.

But more importantly, the soldiers are at (0,0,0) with velocity. This suggests that:
1. They were spawned at the correct position
2. But then something moved them to (0,0,0) — OR
3. They were spawned at (0,0,0) because the rally point position was wrong at spawn time

Let me check the Initialize code flow:
1. `BarracksAttack.Initialize()` is called → calls `SetupRallyPoint()`
2. `SetupRallyPoint()` creates the rally point at `FindDefaultRallyPosition()`
3. The barracks' `Attack()` method is called → calls `SpawnAllSoldiers()` → spawns soldiers at `_rallyPoint.position`

Wait, but the soldiers' `Initialize()` method sets `transform.SetParent(null)`. Could setting the parent to null be moving the soldiers to (0,0,0)?

YES! That's the issue. When the soldier is parented under the tower (which has scale 0.20, 0.20, 1.00), the soldier's localPosition is relative to the parent. When we call `transform.SetParent(null)`, Unity preserves the WORLD position. But wait, actually `SetParent(null)` should keep the world position...

Actually no, `SetParent(null)` preserves the world position. But there's a subtlety: when the parent has a non-identity scale, the child's world position is calculated from its localPosition through the parent's scale. When we unparent, Unity converts the local position to world position, which should be correct.

Hmm, but the soldiers are at (0,0,0). Let me check if maybe the `SetParent(null)` in `Initialize()` is being called BEFORE the spawn position is set. Looking at the code:

```csharp
GameObject soldierObj = Instantiate(_soldierData.soldierPrefab, spawnPos, Quaternion.identity);
// Don't parent under tower — Dynamic Rigidbody2D + scaled parent causes rubber-banding
// (No SetParent call)

Soldier soldier = soldierObj.GetComponent<Soldier>();
...
soldier.Initialize(_soldierData, _rallyPoint);
```

And in `Initialize()`:
```csharp
// Unparent from tower — Dynamic Rigidbody2D under a scaled parent causes rubber-banding
transform.SetParent(null);
```

So the soldier is instantiated with `spawnPos` position, then `SetParent(null)` is called. But the soldier was never parented under the tower (I removed the `SetParent(transform)` line). So `SetParent(null)` is redundant but shouldn't cause issues.

Wait, but the prefab is instantiated with `Instantiate(prefab, spawnPos, Quaternion.identity)`. This creates the object at `spawnPos`. Then `SetParent(null)` is called, which shouldn't change the position (it's already at root level).

But the soldiers are at (0,0,0)... Let me check if maybe the Rigidbody2D is overriding the position. When a Dynamic Rigidbody2D is on a newly instantiated object, it might be at the origin initially and then update.

Actually, wait. The soldier prefab has scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) and a Rigidbody2D. When instantiated at spawnPos, the transform.position is set to spawnPos, but the Rigidbody2D.position might be at (0,0) because the Rigidbody2D was deserialized from the prefab at origin.

Let me check: after `Instantiate(prefab, spawnPos, Quaternion.identity)`, is the Rigidbody2D's position at spawnPos or at (0,0)?

Actually, `Instantiate` should set both transform.position and Rigidbody2D.position to spawnPos. But there might be a timing issue where the Rigidbody2D isn't initialized yet when we check.

Hmm, let me just check the spawn position and the RB position after spawn.</think>士兵在 (0,0,0) 但有速度！可能是 Rigidbody2D 初始化问题。让我检查：

messages.content:
<think>The soldiers are at (0,0,0) even though they should be at the rally point. The rally point is at (0.39, 2.20, -0.42). The tower has scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00).

The problem is now clear: the soldiers are being instantiated at the spawn position (near the rally point), but then `Initialize()` is called which does `transform.SetParent(null)`. However, the soldiers were already NOT parented (I removed the `SetParent(transform)` line). So the issue must be something else.

Wait, let me re-read the code flow:
1. `Instantiate(_soldierData.soldierPrefab, spawnPos, Quaternion.identity)` — creates soldier at spawnPos
2. `soldierObj.transform.SetParent(transform)` was REMOVED
3. `soldier.Initialize(_soldierData, _rallyPoint)` is called
4. Inside `Initialize()`, `transform.SetParent(null)` is called

But wait, the Instantiate call should set the soldier's position to `spawnPos`. Then `Initialize()` is called and `SetParent(null)` shouldn't change the position since the soldier is already at root level.

Unless... the `SetParent(null)` IS the problem. When you call `SetParent(null)` on an object that's already at root level, Unity should be a no-op. But there might be an edge case with the Rigidbody2D.

Actually, I think the issue might be with the prefab itself. The Soldier_Knight prefab has a Rigidbody2D. When you instantiate it with a specific position, the Rigidbody2D might not pick up the position immediately. It might be at (0,0) initially.

Let me check: after Instantiate, does the Rigidbody2D.position match the spawn position?

Actually, I think I found the real issue. The `Instantiate` function with position creates the object and sets `transform.position`. But for a Dynamic Rigidbody2D, the `Rigidbody2D.position` might be initialized from the prefab (which could be at origin), not from the spawn position. This is a known Unity quirk.

The fix is to explicitly set the Rigidbody2D position after instantiation. Let me update the `Initialize` method to set the Rigidbody2D position.

Actually wait, let me re-examine. `Instantiate(prefab, spawnPos, Quaternion.identity)` should set both transform.position and Rigidbody2D.position to spawnPos. This is the standard Unity behavior.

But then `transform.SetParent(null)` in `Initialize()` might cause issues if the parent scale is involved... but the soldier is NOT parented to the tower, so there's no scale issue.

Hmm, let me check if maybe the `Instantiate` with a specific position doesn't work correctly with prefabs that have Rigidbody2D. This might be a Unity bug or a timing issue.

Let me try a different approach: let me check what happens if I manually set the position after instantiation.

Actually, looking at the diagnostic output more carefully:
- All 3 soldiers are at EXACTLY (0,0,0)
- They have no parent (parent=none)
- Their RB position is also (0,0,0)
- But the rally point is at (0.39, 2.20, -0.42)

This is very suspicious. The spawn position should be near the rally point, not at origin. Let me look at the SpawnAllSoldiers code again:

```csharp
Vector3 basePos = _rallyPoint != null ? _rallyPoint.position : transform.position;

for (int i = 0; i < toSpawn; i++)
{
    float angle = (2f * Mathf.PI * i) / _maxSoldiers;
    Vector3 offset = new Vector3(
        Mathf.Cos(angle) * _spawnRadius,
        Mathf.Sin(angle) * _spawnRadius,
        0f
    );
    Vector3 spawnPos = basePos + offset;
    GameObject soldierObj = Instantiate(_soldierData.soldierPrefab, spawnPos, Quaternion.identity);
    ...
}
```

The spawn position is calculated from `_rallyPoint.position` which is (0.39, 2.20, -0.42). So spawnPos should be near that. But the soldiers are at (0,0,0).

Wait, I think the issue might be timing. The `Initialize` method is called AFTER the soldier is created, and inside Initialize, `transform.SetParent(null)` is called. If the prefab's Rigidbody2D was previously at origin and the instantiation correctly set the position, then `SetParent(null)` shouldn't change it.

Unless... hmm, let me check if the Rigidbody2D is somehow resetting the position to the prefab's original position.

Actually, I just realized something. The soldier prefab has:
- Transform at (0,0,0)
- Rigidbody2D at (0,0)
- Scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00)

When we instantiate with position (0.39, 2.20, -0.42), Unity should set:
- Transform.position = (0.39, 2.20, -0.42)
- Rigidbody2D.position = (0.39, 2.20)

But maybe there's a timing issue where the Rigidbody2D hasn't synced yet.

Or maybe the issue is in `Initialize()`:
```csharp
// Unparent from tower — Dynamic Rigidbody2D under a scaled parent causes rubber-banding
transform.SetParent(null);
```

When we call `transform.SetParent(null)`, Unity recalculates the local position from the world position. But for a Dynamic Rigidbody2D, the physics engine might be tracking the position separately. When we change the transform parent, the Rigidbody2D might not update correctly.

Actually, I think the real fix should be:
1. In `Initialize()`, after `SetParent(null)`, explicitly set the Rigidbody2D position
2. OR, don't call `SetParent(null)` in Initialize since the soldier is already unparented

Let me update the Initialize method to properly set the RB position.

Actually, I think the issue is more fundamental. Let me check if `Instantiate(prefab, position, rotation)` actually sets the Rigidbody2D position correctly. Maybe it doesn't.

Let me just fix the code by explicitly setting the RB position in Initialize and SpawnAllSoldiers.</think>士兵都在 (0,0,0)！Rigidbody2D 可能在 `Instantiate` 后没有同步位置。让我修复 `Initialize` 方法，显式设置 RB 位置：

messages.content:
<think>Interesting! Now:
1. The soldiers have velocity (3.56, 1.82) — they ARE trying to move!
2. The rbPos is (0.14, 0.07) — slightly moved from origin
3. But the transformPos is STILL (0, 0, 0)!

Wait, this is very revealing. The Rigidbody2D is moving (rbPos changed from 0,0 to 0.14, 0.07), but the transform.position is stuck at (0,0,0). This is the exact symptom of the parent scale problem!

But wait, the soldiers have `parent=none`. They're not parented to anything. So the scale issue shouldn't apply here.

Unless... the soldier prefab itself has scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00). When the soldier is unparented, its localScale IS its world scale. If the soldier's localScale is (0.20, 0.20, 1.00), then:
- transform.position = (0, 0, 0) in local space (since there's no parent)
- But wait, if there's no parent, local position IS world position

Hmm, this is very strange. Let me check: if `parent=none`, then `transform.position` should equal `transform.localPosition`. And both should match the Rigidbody2D position (approximately).

But the transform.position is (0, 0, 0) while the rb.position is (0.14, 0.07). This means the Rigidbody2D is moving but the transform isn't updating.

This is a classic symptom of a Dynamic Rigidbody2D that has a non-uniform scale. When a Dynamic Rigidbody2D has a scale other than (1,1,1), the physics engine and the transform system can get out of sync.

Wait, but the scale is (0.20, 0.20, 1.00). This is non-uniform (z is different from x/y). Actually, (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) is uniform in XY but not in Z. For 2D physics, the Z scale shouldn't matter, but non-uniform scale in general can cause issues.

Actually, let me reconsider. The Unity documentation says:
"Rigidbody2D only supports uniform scale. When the Transform scale is not uniform, the Rigidbody2D may behave incorrectly."

The soldier's scale is (0.20, 0.20, 1.00). The X and Y are the same (0.20), but the Z is different (1.00). This might be considered "non-uniform" by Unity's Rigidbody2D system.

Wait, actually (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) — the X and Y are equal, which should be fine for 2D physics. The Z scale shouldn't matter. Let me check the actual transform scale of the instantiated soldier.

Actually, I think the fundamental issue might be that the soldier prefab shouldn't have scale (0.20, 0.20, 1.00) on its root transform. The scale should be (1,1,1) and the visual elements should be scaled separately. This is a best practice for objects with Rigidbody2D.

Let me fix this by:

Wait, but that would change the visual appearance. A scale of 0.20 means the soldier sprite is rendered at 20% size. If I change the root to (1,1,1), the soldier would appear 5x larger.

The proper fix is to:
1. Reset root scale to (1,1,1)
2. Adjust the SpriteRenderer's transform or the sprite's pixelsPerUnit to get the correct visual size
3. Adjust the CircleCollider2D radius and other physics parameters

OR, simpler: just don't use Rigidbody2D at all, and use transform.position for movement (since we're in a 2D tower defense game with no complex physics needs).

Actually, the SIMPLEST fix is to just abandon Rigidbody2D entirely and move the soldier via transform.position directly. We don't need physics simulation for soldier movement — they just need to walk towards targets and collide with enemies (which we're handling via code, not physics).

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.
